title: rhinanthus glacialis subsp. simplex
intro: Rhinanthus glacialis subsp. simplex, also known as the glacier broomrape, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae. This parasitic plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rhinanthus glacialis subsp. simplex include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
